brief.speaker: carol
brief.text: Inner join excludes the non-match. I need to see the non-match. Change the join type.

expected.sql: SELECT timestamp, message, service_name, team FROM logs LEFT JOIN services ON logs.tags['service'] = service_name WHERE logs.tags['service'] = 'chrono-portal-mirror' LIMIT 10

hints.text: Carol: 'Zero rows — inner join excluded the phantom again. Try LEFT JOIN.'
Carol: 'Query error. The join type goes between the table names — try LEFT JOIN.'
Carol: 'LEFT JOIN — keeps all rows from the left table, fills NULLs where no match exists.'

success.speaker: carol
success.text: There it is. Null team. Null tier. Null registered_at. Left join doesn't exclude the non-match — it shows it to you, with nothing where the catalog data should be.
